context.goal: Change the currently selected text object's drawing mode from fill to stroke using the Text properties dialog.
context.cues: A TIAEditor window shows slide page 6/15 with a single character in the word 'and' selected in magenta on the document canvas. The top toolbar includes a Change button, and the page content is a bullet list titled 'Core Marketing Concepts'.

state_anchor.locator: magenta-selected character in the word 'and' on the first bullet line

anchors.locator: Text properties dialog > Drawing mode dropdown currently showing 'Fill'

procedure.expect: The Text properties dialog opens over the document, showing fields such as Text to insert, Drawing mode, Font, Font size, Rotation, and color.
The Drawing mode dropdown expands and shows choices including Fill, Stroke, Fill&Stroke, and Invisible.
The Drawing mode field changes to 'Stroke', and the color label updates to border color.
The dialog closes and the selected character's appearance is updated on the slide, leaving the modified text visible in the bullet line.
